objects.name: Copyright 2015-2025, The MITRE Corporation. MITRE ATT&CK and ATT&CK are registered trademarks of The MITRE Corporation.

definition.statement: copyright 2015-2025, the mitre corporation. mitre att&ck and att&ck are registered trademarks of the mitre corporation.

objects.name: Asset
objects.description: Data sources with information about the set of devices found within the network, along with their current software and configurations

objects.name: Operational Databases
objects.description: Operational databases contain information about the status of the operational process and associated devices, including any measurements, events, history, or alarms that have occurred

objects.name: Application Vetting
objects.description: Application vetting report generated by an external cloud service.

objects.name: User Interface
objects.description: Visual activity on the device that could alert the user to potentially malicious behavior.

objects.name: Module
objects.description: Executable files consisting of one or more shared classes and interfaces, such as portable executable (PE) format binaries/dynamic link libraries (DLL), executable and linkable format (ELF) binaries/shared libraries, and Mach-O format binaries/shared libraries(Citation: Microsoft LoadLibrary)(Citation: Microsoft Module Class)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (n.d.). Module Class. Retrieved September 28, 2021.
Microsoft. (2018, December 5). LoadLibraryA function (libloaderapi.h). Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Firewall
objects.description: A network security system, running locally on an endpoint or remotely as a service (ex: cloud environment), that monitors and controls incoming/outgoing network traffic based on predefined rules(Citation: AWS Sec Groups VPC)

external_references.description: Amazon. (n.d.). Security groups for your VPC. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Process
objects.description: Instances of computer programs that are being executed by at least one thread. Processes have memory space for process executables, loaded modules (DLLs or shared libraries), and allocated memory regions containing everything from user input to application-specific data structures(Citation: Microsoft Processes and Threads)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2018, May 31). Processes and Threads. Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Domain Name
objects.description: Information obtained (commonly through registration or activity logs) regarding one or more IP addresses registered with human readable names (ex: mitre.org)

objects.name: Service
objects.description: A computer process that is configured to execute continuously in the background and perform system tasks, in some cases before any user has logged in(Citation: Microsoft Services)(Citation: Linux Services Run Levels)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2017, March 30). Introduction to Windows Service Applications. Retrieved September 28, 2021.
The Linux Foundation. (2006, January 11). An introduction to services, runlevels, and rc.d scripts. Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Active Directory
objects.description: A database and set of services that allows administrators to manage permissions, access to network resources, and stored data objects (user, group, application, or devices)(Citation: Microsoft AD DS Getting Started)

external_references.description: Foulds, I. et al. (2018, August 7). AD DS Getting Started. Retrieved September 23, 2021.

objects.name: Scheduled Job
objects.description: Automated tasks that can be executed at a specific time or on a recurring schedule running in the background (ex: Cron daemon, task scheduler, BITS)(Citation: Microsoft Tasks)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2018, May 31). Tasks. Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Firmware
objects.description: Computer software that provides low-level control for the hardware and device(s) of a host, such as BIOS or UEFI/EFI

objects.name: Cluster
objects.description: A set of containerized computing resources that are managed together but have separate nodes to execute various tasks and/or applications(Citation: Kube Cluster Admin)(Citation: Kube Cluster Info)

external_references.description: kubernetes. (2021, January 16). Cluster Administration. Retrieved October 13, 2021.
kubernetes. (n.d.). cluster-info. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Network Traffic
objects.description: Data transmitted across a network (ex: Web, DNS, Mail, File, etc.), that is either summarized (ex: Netflow) and/or captured as raw data in an analyzable format (ex: PCAP)

objects.name: Network Share
objects.description: A storage resource (typically a folder or drive) made available from one host to others using network protocols, such as Server Message Block (SMB) or Network File System (NFS)(Citation: Microsoft NFS Overview)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2018, July 9). Network File System overview. Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Cloud Service
objects.description: Infrastructure, platforms, or software that are hosted on-premise or by third-party providers, made available to users through network connections and/or APIs(Citation: Amazon AWS)(Citation: Azure Products)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (n.d.). Azure products. Retrieved November 17, 2024.
Amazon. (n.d.). Start Building on AWS Today. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Malware Repository
objects.description: Information obtained (via shared or submitted samples) regarding malicious software (droppers, backdoors, etc.) used by adversaries

objects.name: Volume
objects.description: Block object storage hosted on-premise or by third-party providers, typically made available to resources as virtualized hard drives(Citation: Amazon S3)(Citation: Azure Blob Storage)(Citation: Google Cloud Storage)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (n.d.). Azure Blob Storage. Retrieved November 17, 2024.
Amazon. (n.d.). Amazon S3. Retrieved October 13, 2021.
Google. (n.d.). Cloud Storage. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Driver
objects.description: A computer program that operates or controls a particular type of device that is attached to a computer. Provides a software interface to hardware devices, enabling operating systems and other computer programs to access hardware functions without needing to know precise details about the hardware being used(Citation: IOKit Fundamentals)(Citation: Windows Getting Started Drivers)

external_references.description: Apple. (2014, April 9). What Is the I/O Kit?. Retrieved September 24, 2021.
Viviano, A. (2021, August 17). Getting started with Windows drivers: User mode and kernel mode. Retrieved September 24, 2021.

objects.name: Kernel
objects.description: A computer program, at the core of a computer OS, that resides in memory and facilitates interactions between hardware and software components(Citation: STIG Audit Kernel Modules)(Citation: Init Man Page)

external_references.description: Kerrisk, M. (2021, March 22). INIT_MODULE(2). Retrieved September 28, 2021.
Unified Compliance Framework. (2016, December 20). The audit system must be configured to audit the loading and unloading of dynamic kernel modules.. Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Command
objects.description: A directive given to a computer program, acting as an interpreter of some kind, in order to perform a specific task(Citation: Confluence Linux Command  Line)(Citation: Audit OSX)

external_references.description: Confluence Support. (2021, September 8). How to enable command line audit logging in linux. Retrieved September 23, 2021.
Gagliardi, R. (n.d.). Audit in a OS X System. Retrieved September 23, 2021.

objects.name: Snapshot
objects.description: A point-in-time copy of cloud volumes (files, settings, etc.) that can be created and/or deployed in cloud environments(Citation: Microsoft Snapshot)(Citation: Amazon Snapshots)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2021, September 16). Create a snapshot of a virtual hard disk. Retrieved October 13, 2021.
Amazon. (n.d.). Amazon EBS snapshots. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Drive
objects.description: A non-volatile data storage device (hard drive, floppy disk, USB flash drive) with at least one formatted partition, typically mounted to the file system and/or assigned a drive letter(Citation: Sysmon EID 9)

external_references.description: Russinovich, R. & Garnier, T. (2021, August 18). Sysmon Event ID 9. Retrieved September 24, 2021.

objects.name: File
objects.description: A computer resource object, managed by the I/O system, for storing data (such as images, text, videos, computer programs, or any wide variety of other media).(Citation: Microsoft File Mgmt)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2018, May 31). File Management (Local File Systems). Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Sensor Health
objects.description: Information from host telemetry providing insights about system status, errors, or other notable functional activity

objects.name: Instance
objects.description: A virtual server environment which runs workloads, hosted on-premise or by third-party cloud providers(Citation: Amazon VM)(Citation: Google VM)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (n.d.). What is a virtual machine (VM)?. Retrieved November 17, 2024.
Google. (n.d.). Virtual machine instances. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Logon Session
objects.description: Logon occurring on a system or resource (local, domain, or cloud) to which a user/device is gaining access after successful authentication and authorization(Citation: Microsoft Audit Logon Events)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2021, September 6). Audit logon events. Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Application Log
objects.description: Events collected by third-party services such as mail servers, web applications, or other appliances (not by the native OS or platform)(Citation: Confluence Logs)

external_references.description: Confluence Support. (2021, April 22). Working with Confluence Logs. Retrieved September 23, 2021.

objects.name: Group
objects.description: A collection of multiple user accounts that share the same access rights to the computer and/or network resources and have common security rights(Citation: Amazon IAM Groups)

external_references.description: Amazon. (n.d.). IAM user groups. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Persona
objects.description: A malicious online profile representing a user commonly used by adversaries to social engineer or otherwise target victims

objects.name: Cloud Storage
objects.description: Data object storage infrastructure hosted on-premise or by third-party providers, made available to users through network connections and/or APIs(Citation: Amazon S3)(Citation: Azure Blob Storage)(Citation: Google Cloud Storage)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (n.d.). Azure Blob Storage. Retrieved November 17, 2024.
Amazon. (n.d.). Amazon S3. Retrieved October 13, 2021.
Google. (n.d.). Cloud Storage. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Internet Scan
objects.description: Information obtained (commonly via active network traffic probes or web crawling) regarding various types of resources and servers connected to the public Internet

objects.name: WMI
objects.description: The infrastructure for management data and operations that enables local and remote management of Windows personal computers and servers(Citation: Microsoft WMI System Classes)(Citation: Microsoft WMI Architecture)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2018, May 31). WMI System Classes. Retrieved September 29, 2021.
Microsoft. (2018, May 31). WMI Architecture. Retrieved September 29, 2021.

objects.name: Named Pipe
objects.description: Mechanisms that allow inter-process communication locally or over the network. A named pipe is usually found as a file and processes attach to it(Citation: Microsoft Named Pipes)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2018, May 31). Named Pipes. Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Certificate
objects.description: A digital document, which highlights information such as the owner's identity, used to instill trust in public keys used while encrypting network communications

objects.name: Web Credential
objects.description: Credential material, such as session cookies or tokens, used to authenticate to web applications and services(Citation: Medium Authentication Tokens)(Citation: Auth0 Access Tokens)

external_references.description: Auth0. (n.d.). Access Tokens. Retrieved September 29, 2021.
Hsu, S. (2018, June 30). Session vs Token Based Authentication. Retrieved September 29, 2021.

objects.name: Script
objects.description: A file or stream containing a list of commands, allowing them to be launched in sequence(Citation: Microsoft PowerShell Logging)(Citation: FireEye PowerShell Logging)(Citation: Microsoft AMSI)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2020, March 30). about_Logging_Windows. Retrieved September 28, 2021.
Dunwoody, M. (2016, February 11). Greater Visibility Through PowerShell Logging. Retrieved September 28, 2021.
Microsoft. (2019, April 19). Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI). Retrieved September 28, 2021.

objects.name: Image
objects.description: A single file used to deploy a virtual machine/bootable disk into an on-premise or third-party cloud environment(Citation: Microsoft Image)(Citation: Amazon AMI)

external_references.description: Amazon. (n.d.). Amazon Machine Images (AMI). Retrieved October 13, 2021.
Microsoft. (2021, August 23). Create a managed image of a generalized VM in Azure. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: Windows Registry
objects.description: A Windows OS hierarchical database that stores much of the information and settings for software programs, hardware devices, user preferences, and operating-system configurations(Citation: Microsoft Registry)

external_references.description: Microsoft. (2018, May 31). Registry. Retrieved September 29, 2021.

objects.name: Container
objects.description: A standard unit of virtualized software that packages up code and all its dependencies so the application runs quickly and reliably from one computing environment to another(Citation: Docker Docs Container)

external_references.description: docker docs. (n.d.). Containers. Retrieved October 13, 2021.

objects.name: User Account
objects.description: A profile representing a user, device, service, or application used to authenticate and access resources

objects.name: Pod
objects.description: A single unit of shared resources within a cluster, comprised of one or more containers(Citation: Kube Kubectl)(Citation: Kube Pod)

external_references.description: kubernetes. (n.d.). kubectl. Retrieved October 13, 2021.
kubenetes. (n.d.). Pod v1 core. Retrieved October 13, 2021.
